guardrails: Theorem A (rows&cols xor 0 => deg L <= 2) is proved; its mechanism is verified as a finite ANF identity.
Lemma B (magic => rows&cols xor 0) is finite-certified on the 880; only its least-significant bit is derived (from even line sums).
The diagonals are essential (B-neg witness); semi-magic is not enough.
No public record-level widening or universal claim beyond order-4 normal magic squares is made.

metadata.description: deg(L) <= 2 is derived from rows-xor-0 and cols-xor-0 (Theorem A), whose hypothesis is finite-certified on all 880 magic squares (Lemma B); diagonals are essential (B-neg witness).

summary.consequence: Direction-lemma hypothesis H1 (deg L <= 2) is now reduced to the transparent GF(2) line condition (Lemma B) and proved from it (Theorem A). Only Lemma B's bits 1-3 remain a certified input.
summary.open_residue: Bits 1-3 of Lemma B (rows/cols xor 0 beyond the LSB) are not yet derived from the full row+column+diagonal magic system; they are finite-certified on the 880. B-neg shows the diagonals are necessary, so any derivation must use them.
